---
title: Graphs
description: Vertices, edges, and network structure.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Define vertices and edges.
- Recognize directed and undirected graphs.
- Connect graphs to networks.
:::

A graph is a collection of vertices connected by edges. Graphs model networks:
web pages, roads, dependencies, and relationships.

::: {.practice-box}
1. What is a vertex?
2. What is an edge?
3. Give one real-world network modeled by a graph.
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: a node or object in a graph.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: a connection between two vertices.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: examples include roads between cities, friendships between people, web
links between pages, or dependencies between tasks.
:::
